description: Move sandbox state to a global directory, introduce a registry of named sandbox instances, refactor providers to accept ConnectInfo, add batch creation, auto-shutdown, cost tracking, and preserve backward compatibility through automatic migration.

userStories.title: Global Directory Resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want sandbox state stored in a global directory so that sandboxes are accessible from any project directory.
acceptanceCriteria: GlobalDir() resolves $HAL_CONFIG_HOME → $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/hal → ~/.config/hal in that order
SandboxesDir() returns GlobalDir()/sandboxes/
EnsureGlobalDir() creates both directories with 0700 permissions if missing
EnsureGlobalDir() is idempotent — safe to call repeatedly
Tests use t.Setenv("HAL_CONFIG_HOME", tmpDir) for isolation
File: internal/sandbox/global.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: UUIDv7 Generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a UUIDv7 generator with injectable clock and random source so that sandbox IDs are unique, monotonically ordered, and testable.
acceptanceCriteria: UUIDSource struct accepts injectable clock func() time.Time and rand io.Reader
NewV7() produces RFC 9562-compliant UUIDv7 strings
Uses crypto/rand by default with proper error handling (returns error, not panic)
Tests verify: format (8-4-4-4-12 hex), version bits (nibble = 0x7), variant bits (top 2 = 0b10), monotonic ordering across sequential calls, and rand-failure error path
No external UUID library dependency
File: internal/sandbox/uuid.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Name Validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a canonical name validator so that sandbox names are safe for filenames, cloud resources, and Tailscale hostnames.
acceptanceCriteria: ValidateName(name) enforces: 1–59 chars, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, no leading/trailing/consecutive hyphens
Returns specific error messages: "must be 1-59 chars", "must be lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens", "must not start or end with hyphen", "must not contain consecutive hyphens"
TailscaleHostname(name) returns "hal-" + name
SandboxNameFromBranch(branch) sanitizes a git branch name into a valid sandbox name (lowercase, replace invalid chars with hyphens, trim)
Table-driven tests cover: valid names, empty, too long (60 chars), uppercase, special chars, leading/trailing hyphens, consecutive hyphens, exactly 59 chars (boundary)
File: internal/sandbox/name.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Batch Name Generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want batch name generation so that --count N produces correctly padded, validated names.
acceptanceCriteria: BatchNames(base, count) returns []string of {base}-01 through {base}-N
Zero-padding width is max(2, digits(count)): count=5 → -01..-05, count=100 → -001..-100
Each generated name is validated via ValidateName; returns error if any name would be invalid
Returns error if count < 1
Returns error if base name + padding suffix would exceed 59 chars
File: internal/sandbox/name.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sandbox State Type
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an expanded SandboxState struct so that each sandbox instance carries identity, networking, lifecycle, and config fields.
acceptanceCriteria: SandboxState struct has all fields: ID, Name, Provider, WorkspaceID, IP, TailscaleIP, TailscaleHostname, Status, CreatedAt, StoppedAt, AutoShutdown, IdleHours, Size, Repo, SnapshotID
JSON tags match spec exactly (camelCase, omitempty where specified)
Status values are string constants: StatusRunning = "running", StatusStopped = "stopped", StatusUnknown = "unknown"
File: internal/sandbox/types.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Registry CRUD Operations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want registry functions to save, load, list, and remove sandbox instances from the global directory.
acceptanceCriteria: SaveInstance(instance *SandboxState) writes to sandboxes/{name}.json with 0600 permissions using temp-file + rename for atomicity
LoadInstance(name) reads and unmarshals sandboxes/{name}.json; when missing, returned error satisfies errors.Is(err, fs.ErrNotExist)
ListInstances() returns all SandboxState from sandboxes/*.json, sorted by name
RemoveInstance(name) deletes sandboxes/{name}.json, returns error if not found
ForceWriteInstance(instance) overwrites without collision check (used by --force)
Name collision: SaveInstance returns error 'sandbox "X" already exists' if file exists
Tests use t.Setenv("HAL_CONFIG_HOME", tmpDir) for isolation
File: internal/sandbox/registry.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Default Sandbox Resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want auto-resolution logic so that commands work without a name argument when exactly one sandbox exists.
acceptanceCriteria: ResolveDefault(filter func(*SandboxState) bool) returns the single matching instance, or error
If 0 match: error "no sandboxes found" (or "no running sandboxes" if filter is status-based)
If >1 match: error listing available names as choices (e.g., "multiple sandboxes found: api-backend, frontend, worker-01")
If exactly 1 match: returns instance + hint message "connecting to only active sandbox \"X\""
File: internal/sandbox/registry.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Global Config Type and Load/Save
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a global sandbox config type so that provider settings, defaults, and env vars are stored in ~/.config/hal/sandbox-config.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: GlobalConfig struct covers all fields: Provider, Defaults (AutoShutdown, IdleHours), Env map, TailscaleLockdown, and per-provider sections (Daytona, DigitalOcean, Hetzner, Lightsail)
LoadGlobalConfig() reads sandbox-config.yaml from GlobalDir(), returns DefaultGlobalConfig() if file missing
SaveGlobalConfig(cfg) writes with 0600 permissions using temp-file + rename
DefaultGlobalConfig() returns provider "daytona", autoShutdown true, idleHours 48
Uses pointer fields for YAML unmarshal to distinguish missing vs explicit empty (following rawAutoConfig pattern)
Tests use t.Setenv("HAL_CONFIG_HOME", tmpDir) for isolation
File: internal/sandbox/globalconfig.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migration Logic — Config File
userStories.description: As a developer, I want project sandbox config migration to global config to follow clear conflict rules.
acceptanceCriteria: If local project sandbox config exists and global sandbox-config.yaml is missing, Migrate(projectDir) copies local config to global path
If both local and global config files exist, global config remains unchanged and local config is preserved
If local project sandbox config is missing, migration performs no config write
Config migration behavior is covered with table-driven tests for the three cases
File: internal/sandbox/migrate.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auto-Migration Helper and First Adoption
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shared auto-migration helper so commands can opt into migration with consistent warning behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Add helper runSandboxAutoMigrate(projectDir string, out io.Writer) that calls sandbox.Migrate(projectDir)
If sandbox.Migrate returns an error, helper writes warning exactly: warning: sandbox migration failed: <error> and returns nil
If sandbox.Migrate succeeds with no actions, helper writes no output
hal sandbox start calls runSandboxAutoMigrate before create logic
File: cmd/sandbox.go and cmd/sandbox_start.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: ConnectInfo Type and SandboxResult Contract
userStories.description: As a developer, I want connection-data primitives defined before provider signature migration.
acceptanceCriteria: ConnectInfo struct defines fields: Name, IP, WorkspaceID
ConnectInfoFromState(instance *SandboxState) maps Name, WorkspaceID, and PreferredIP(instance)
PreferredIP(instance *SandboxState) returns TailscaleIP when non-empty, otherwise IP
SandboxResult explicitly defines fields required for persistence: ID, Name, IP, TailscaleIP
Provider interface method signatures remain unchanged in this story
File: internal/sandbox/provider.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor Daytona Provider to ConnectInfo
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Daytona provider updated to use ConnectInfo so that it no longer accesses state files.
acceptanceCriteria: SSH uses info.Name (Daytona resolves by workspace name)
Exec uses info.Name
Stop uses info.Name
Delete uses info.Name
Status uses info.Name
No references to StateDir or LoadState() remain
Existing Daytona tests pass or are updated to match new signatures
File: internal/sandbox/provider_daytona.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor Hetzner Provider to ConnectInfo
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Hetzner provider updated to use ConnectInfo so that it no longer accesses state files.
acceptanceCriteria: SSH uses info.IP with user root
Exec uses info.IP with user root
Stop uses info.Name (Hetzner server name)
Delete uses info.Name
Status uses info.Name
All StateDir fields and LoadState() calls removed
Existing Hetzner tests pass or are updated to match new signatures
File: internal/sandbox/provider_hetzner.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor DigitalOcean Provider to ConnectInfo
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the DigitalOcean provider updated to use ConnectInfo so that it no longer accesses state files.
acceptanceCriteria: SSH uses info.IP with user root
Exec uses info.IP with user root
Stop uses info.WorkspaceID (droplet ID)
Delete uses info.WorkspaceID
Status uses info.WorkspaceID
resolveDropletTarget() and refreshIP() removed
All StateDir fields and LoadState() calls removed
Existing DigitalOcean tests pass or are updated to match new signatures
File: internal/sandbox/provider_digitalocean.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor Lightsail Provider to ConnectInfo
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Lightsail provider updated to use ConnectInfo so that it no longer accesses state files.
acceptanceCriteria: SSH uses info.IP with user ubuntu
Exec uses info.IP with user ubuntu
Stop uses info.Name (Lightsail instance name)
Delete uses info.Name
Status uses info.Name
All StateDir fields and LoadState() calls removed
Existing Lightsail tests pass or are updated to match new signatures
File: internal/sandbox/provider_lightsail.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Global Sandbox Setup Command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal sandbox setup to use global paths so setup is not project-scoped.
acceptanceCriteria: hal sandbox setup works when no .hal/ directory exists in the current working directory
Setup saves to ~/.config/hal/sandbox-config.yaml through SaveGlobalConfig
Secrets in global config are written with 0600 permissions
Command logic is extracted into testable runSandboxSetup(in io.Reader, out io.Writer) helper
Tests verify setup from a non-hal directory writes the global config file
File: cmd/sandbox.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Single Sandbox Start — Create and Register
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal sandbox start -n NAME to create one sandbox and register it in the global registry.
acceptanceCriteria: hal sandbox start -n NAME calls provider.Create exactly once and persists exactly one SandboxState entry on success
Persisted SandboxState sets: ID (UUIDv7), Name, Provider, WorkspaceID, IP, CreatedAt, and Status="running"
When -n is omitted, name defaults from current git branch via SandboxNameFromBranch
When --size is not set, provider size uses the default from global config
File: cmd/sandbox_start.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auto-Shutdown Env Injection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want auto-shutdown configuration injected as env vars at sandbox creation so that cloud-init can configure idle timers.
acceptanceCriteria: When autoShutdown is true: HAL_AUTO_SHUTDOWN=true and HAL_IDLE_HOURS={hours} injected into creation env
When autoShutdown is false: HAL_AUTO_SHUTDOWN=false injected, no HAL_IDLE_HOURS
--auto-shutdown flag overrides config default (true)
--no-auto-shutdown flag sets autoShutdown to false
--idle-hours flag overrides config default (48)
Persisted SandboxState stores resolved AutoShutdown and IdleHours values used for creation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Batch Creation with --count — Planning and Preflight
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal sandbox start -n BASE --count N to validate the full batch plan before execution begins.
acceptanceCriteria: --count N generates target names via BatchNames(base, N) before any provider.Create call
Preflight validates every generated name is unique and does not already exist in the global registry
If preflight fails for any target, command returns an error and provider.Create is not called for any sandbox
Execution is delegated to a dedicated helper after preflight succeeds
File: cmd/sandbox_start.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Start Command Flag Wiring
userStories.description: As a developer, I want start flags wired so size, repo, and env overrides work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: --size overrides provider size from config
--repo stores repo label in SandboxState (informational only)
-e, --env KEY=VALUE appends extra env vars (can be specified multiple times)
All wired flags have short descriptions in Cobra help text
Extracted into testable runSandboxStart helper with sandboxStartDeps struct for test injection
File: cmd/sandbox_start.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cost Estimation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want embedded hourly rates and cost estimation so that list can show per-sandbox and total costs.
acceptanceCriteria: hourlyRates map covers: digitalocean (3 sizes), hetzner (3 sizes), lightsail (3 sizes)
EstimatedCost(instance *SandboxState) returns hours_since_creation x hourly_rate
Unknown provider or size returns -1
Cost always accrues from CreatedAt (stopped sandboxes still charge)
Accepts injectable now func() time.Time for deterministic tests
File: internal/sandbox/pricing.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: List Command — Table Output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal sandbox list to render a clear default table view from local state.
acceptanceCriteria: Default output table columns are exactly: NAME, PROVIDER, STATUS, TAILSCALE, AGE, AUTO-OFF, EST.COST
EST.COST displays $X.XX for known rates and — when rate data is unavailable
Summary line format is exact: "N sandboxes (X running, Y stopped)  •  Est. total: $Z.ZZ"
Default list path reads local registry data only and does not call provider APIs
File: cmd/sandbox_list.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: List Command — JSON Output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal sandbox list --json to output machine-readable JSON following the sandbox-list-v1 contract.
acceptanceCriteria: --json outputs the structure: contractVersion, sandboxes[], totals
Required fields on each sandbox: id, name, provider, status, createdAt
Optional fields use omitempty
totals includes total, running, stopped, estimatedCost
JSON output goes to stdout; no other text mixed in
File: cmd/sandbox_list.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: List Command — Live Status Query
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal sandbox list --live to fetch fresh provider status before rendering output.
acceptanceCriteria: With --live, list queries each sandbox provider for current status before rendering
Each provider query has a 10s timeout; timeout result renders status as "unknown" for that sandbox
Successful live query data (status and IP when available) is reflected in rendered output
Live query logic is extracted into a dedicated helper used by list
File: cmd/sandbox_list.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Stop Command — Target Resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal sandbox stop to resolve targets from names, --all, --pattern, and no-arg auto-resolve rules before execution.
acceptanceCriteria: hal sandbox stop NAME [NAME...] resolves explicit names as targets
hal sandbox stop --all resolves all running sandboxes; hal sandbox stop --pattern "worker-*" resolves matching names
hal sandbox stop with no args and no flags follows exact rules: 1 running => select it and print hint, 0 running => error, >1 running => error listing choices
Resolved targets are de-duplicated and sorted by name before execution
Target resolution is implemented in a dedicated helper used by runSandboxStop
File: cmd/sandbox_stop.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Delete Command — Target Resolution and Confirmation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal sandbox delete to resolve targets consistently and handle --all confirmation before execution.
acceptanceCriteria: hal sandbox delete NAME [NAME...] resolves explicit names as targets
hal sandbox delete --all resolves all sandboxes; hal sandbox delete --pattern "worker-*" resolves matching names
hal sandbox delete with no args and no flags follows exact rules: 1 sandbox => select it and print hint, 0 sandboxes => error, >1 sandbox => error listing choices
When --all is used without --yes, command prompts exactly "Delete all sandboxes? [y/N]" and aborts unless user confirms yes
Target resolution and confirmation are implemented in a dedicated helper used by runSandboxDelete
File: cmd/sandbox_delete.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SSH Command — ConnectInfo and Auto-Resolve
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal sandbox ssh to build ConnectInfo from the registry and auto-connect when only one sandbox exists.
acceptanceCriteria: hal sandbox ssh NAME loads instance from registry, builds ConnectInfo with PreferredIP, calls provider.SSH(info)
hal sandbox ssh NAME -- COMMAND passes command to provider.Exec(info, args)
hal sandbox ssh (no name) auto-resolves: if exactly 1 → connect + hint "Connecting to only active sandbox \"X\"..."; if 0 → error; if >1 → error listing choices
File: cmd/sandbox_ssh.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Status Command — Live Query and List Alias
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal sandbox status NAME to show detailed live info, and hal sandbox status (no args) to alias to list.
acceptanceCriteria: hal sandbox status NAME queries provider for live status and displays detailed info (all SandboxState fields)
hal sandbox status (no args) delegates to hal sandbox list behavior
File: cmd/sandbox_status.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migrate Command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal sandbox migrate as a standalone command that invokes migration and streams migration output.
acceptanceCriteria: hal sandbox migrate calls sandbox.Migrate(projectDir) and forwards migration output to the command writer
Command is non-interactive and does not prompt for input
When migration reports no actions, command prints exactly "Nothing to migrate"
When sandbox.Migrate returns an error, command returns that error
File: cmd/sandbox_migrate.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Doctor Check for Legacy State
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a doctor check that warns about unmigrated .hal/sandbox.json and offers hal sandbox migrate as remediation.
acceptanceCriteria: Check ID: legacy_sandbox_state
Scope: migration
Severity: warn
Fires when .hal/sandbox.json exists in current project directory
Message: Legacy .hal/sandbox.json found — run 'hal sandbox migrate'
Remediation: { Command: "hal sandbox migrate", Safe: true }
hal repair can auto-execute this remediation
File: internal/doctor/ (new check)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Machine-Readable Contract Documentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a docs/contracts/sandbox-list-v1.md documenting the JSON contract so that consumers have a stable API reference.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/contracts/sandbox-list-v1.md contains: contract version, full JSON example, field descriptions, required vs optional field lists
Example JSON matches spec exactly
File exists and is valid markdown
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Field-Lock and Doc-Sync Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want contract tests that lock JSON field names and verify doc-code sync so that the machine-readable API cannot drift.
acceptanceCriteria: Field-lock test in cmd/machine_contracts_test.go marshals sandbox list response and asserts required JSON keys by exact name
Round-trip marshal/unmarshal test encodes, decodes, and asserts structural equality
Doc-sync test in cmd/contracts_doc_test.go reads docs/contracts/sandbox-list-v1.md and verifies each required field name appears in the doc
Failing assertions include the missing or mismatched field names in the test output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CLI Metadata and Help Text
userStories.description: As a developer, I want new sandbox commands to include complete Cobra metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: hal sandbox list and hal sandbox migrate each define Use, Short, Long, and Example
Each Example includes the full command path
Command tests assert metadata presence for list and migrate commands
File updates are limited to command definitions and command tests
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migration State Safety and Idempotency
userStories.description: As a developer, I want migration writes to be durable and repeatable so that users cannot lose sandbox state during upgrade.
acceptanceCriteria: Local .hal/sandbox.json is deleted only after global registry save succeeds and a read-back confirms migrated entries are present
If read-back verification fails, migration returns an error and local .hal/sandbox.json remains unchanged
Migration uses copy+remove helpers for cross-device safety instead of direct os.Rename
Running Migrate(projectDir) repeatedly after a successful migration is a no-op
When io.Writer is provided, migration emits one line per action; when writer is nil, migration emits no output
File: internal/sandbox/migrate.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Start Command Collision and Force Replace
userStories.description: As a developer, I want explicit collision and --force semantics so start behavior is predictable.
acceptanceCriteria: Before create, start checks for an existing sandbox with the same name in the global registry
Without --force, an existing name returns error exactly: sandbox "<name>" already exists
With --force, start calls provider.Delete for the existing sandbox and removes its registry entry before creating a new sandbox
If force-delete fails, start returns an error and does not call provider.Create
Successful force-replace creates a new registry entry with the same name and a new ID
File: cmd/sandbox_start.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Batch Creation Execution and Exit Semantics
userStories.description: As a developer, I want batch creation execution and reporting behavior defined so users get clear results.
acceptanceCriteria: After preflight passes, provider.Create runs concurrently for all targets using errgroup
Each target emits exactly one progress line in either success or error format
Only successful creations are persisted to the global registry
Summary line format is exact: "<success>/<total> created (<failed> failed). Failed sandboxes were not registered."
Command exits with code 1 when any target fails, and code 0 when all targets succeed
File: cmd/sandbox_start.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Stop Command Execution and Registry Updates
userStories.description: As a developer, I want stop execution behavior defined so provider calls and registry updates are consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: For each resolved target, command calls provider.Stop then updates registry fields Status="stopped" and StoppedAt=now
Multiple targets are stopped concurrently using errgroup
Command prints one result line per target, including target name and success/failure
If one or more targets fail, command exits non-zero while preserving successful updates
File: cmd/sandbox_stop.go
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Delete Command Execution and Registry Updates
userStories.description: As a developer, I want delete execution behavior defined so provider deletion and local state removal stay ordered and safe.
acceptanceCriteria: For each resolved target, command calls provider.Delete before removing the target from the global registry
If provider.Delete fails for a target, that target remains in the registry
Multiple targets are deleted concurrently using errgroup
Command prints one result line per target, including target name and success/failure
If one or more targets fail, command exits non-zero while preserving successful removals
File: cmd/sandbox_delete.go
Typecheck passes
